title: SDSS Night-Sky Residual Quality Audit
question: Do known night-sky wavelength regions show elevated uncertainty-normalised residual structure relative to matched local control windows?
gap: A released-product QA audit of SDSS DR17 spectra; not a new sky-subtraction pipeline or raw reduction.
novelty: A released-product QA audit; not a new SDSS sky-subtraction pipeline or a replacement for the idlspec2d reduction.
validationContract: mask sensitivity (rerun excluding SPPIXMASK sky-quality bits)
shifted-window null test (no true sky line, expect ratio near 1)
methodology: For each spectrum, a low-order ivar-weighted polynomial continuum is fit to a baseline region surrounding (but excluding) each documented night-sky window ([OI] 5577A, NaD 5890A, [OI] 6300/6364A, OH airglow forest onset ~7600A) and its matched local control window of identical width. Per-pixel uncertainty-normalised residuals r=(flux-continuum)*sqrt(ivar) are aggregated per window into a robust (MAD-based) scale statistic. The sky-vs-control excess statistic is the ratio and difference of these scales; a ratio > 1 indicates elevated residual structure inside the sky window relative to its control. Results are aggregated across spectra (median), bootstrapped for uncertainty, and stratified by SDSS object class and S/N.
assumptions: SDSS/BOSS spec-*.fits COADD wavelengths are vacuum Angstroms (wavelength = 10**loglam), verified against the official SDSS DR17 spectro_basics documentation.
SPPIXMASK bit definitions (NOPLUG, BADTRACE, ..., NOSKY, BRIGHTSKY, BADSKYCHI, REDMONSTER) were verified live against the SDSS DR17 bitmask documentation, not recalled from memory.

Sky-quality SPPIXMASK bits are deliberately not excluded by the default good-pixel mask, since they are themselves partly the phenomenon under study; a mask-sensitivity check reruns with them excluded.

Night-sky line centre wavelengths (5577.3A, 5890/5896A, 6300.3/6363.8A, OH forest onset ~7200A) are the commonly quoted round values documented in docs/ASSUMPTIONS_AND_LIMITATIONS.md, not atlas-precision measurements from Hanuschik (2003).
limitations: A bounded, released-product QA audit; not a new sky-subtraction pipeline, not a substitute for idlspec2d/DESI Guy et al. (2023) pipeline internals.

Continuum is a low-order local polynomial, not a physically motivated stellar/galaxy/QSO template fit; genuine astrophysical features near a sky window could bias the local continuum estimate for that spectrum.
Sample sizes for the real-data run are necessarily small (bounded by an explicit, deterministic query), so per-class/per-SNR stratified results carry wide uncertainty.
reports/report.tex could not be compiled to PDF in this environment (no local LaTeX toolchain); only structural completeness was verified.

citation.author: Biswajit Jana
citation.license: BSD-3-Clause (original code); SDSS DR17 data retain SDSS's own public data-use terms
